name: Montmaurin Loop
description: Cycle Around Montmaurin on a moderate 15.6-mile route, gaining 1205 feet of elevation through vineyards and charming villages.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Charlas features a landscape characterized by rolling hills, rural roads, and agricultural areas, providing varied terrain for cyclists. The region includes paths alongside waterways, offering flatter sections, alongside routes with more significant elevation changes. These routes traverse a mix of open countryside and smaller villages, suitable for exploring by bike. Best touring cycling routes around Charlas The most popular touring cycling route is Montmaurin Loop, a 15.6 miles (25.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete, featuring moderate climbs through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tour of Aurignac loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas, a difficult 21.6 miles (34.8 km) path. This route offers more challenging ascents and panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Charlas is defined by rolling hills, quiet country roads, and occasional canal paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Charlas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Charlas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Charlas?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Charlas, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. These include 4 easy routes, 27 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike routes in Charlas?
A: The touring cycling routes around Charlas feature a landscape characterized by rolling hills, quiet rural roads, and agricultural areas. You'll find paths alongside waterways offering flatter sections, as well as routes with more significant elevation changes. The terrain is a mix of open countryside and smaller villages.

Q: Are there any easy touring bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Charlas?
A: Yes, Charlas offers several easy touring bike trails. For a relatively flat and scenic ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36516964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along canal – Arboretum loop from Larroque&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 13.5 km and is rated as easy. This route provides a gentle introduction to the region's waterways and natural beauty.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Charlas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Charlas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills to quiet country roads, and the opportunity to explore charming rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes in the Charlas area?
A: Yes, many routes in Charlas are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930577722/montmaurin-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montmaurin Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.1 km trail that takes you through the local countryside with moderate climbs. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37608662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.3 km ride through rural landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks to see along the touring bike routes?
A: While cycling around Charlas, you can discover several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6511521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardeilhac Arboretum&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2628276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallo-Roman villa at Montmaurin&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3841663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lieoux church&lt;/a&gt;. For a glimpse into local life, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2011316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boulogne-sur-Gesse Church Square&lt;/a&gt; offers a charming stop.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring bike routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20528012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Aurignac loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.8 km path featuring challenging ascents and panoramic views of the surrounding area. Another longer option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15015504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route La Barthe (Eoux) loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 48.3 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Charlas?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Charlas. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, but it's advisable to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there routes that pass by waterways or canals?
A: Yes, some routes in Charlas include paths alongside waterways, providing flatter sections and scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36516964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along canal – Arboretum loop from Larroque&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a pleasant ride along a canal. You might also encounter sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1816694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Franquevielle Canal from the Cardeilhac Arboretum&lt;/a&gt; on various routes.

Q: Are there any touring bike routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Charlas traverse rolling hills, which naturally provide elevated sections with good viewpoints over the agricultural landscapes and surrounding countryside. The more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20528012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Aurignac loop from Cassagnabère-Tournas&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly known for offering panoramic views after their ascents.
tips: Very beautiful coast between Montmaurin and Sarremezan. Moderate slopes, this coast winds through a small forest and is very pleasant to climb or descend. Very little traffic.
Small village surrounded by the beautiful surrounding countryside and beautiful small roads without traffic. Here you can take your coffee break!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Around Montmaurin touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route usually takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Montmaurin is generally slightly hilly with rolling roads, typical of the Gers region. While mostly well-paved, some segments might include unpaved sections. You'll experience green hills and picturesque countryside.

faqs.question: Is the Around Montmaurin route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this route requires good fitness due to its distance and elevation gain of approximately 367 meters. While not overly strenuous, it's more suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners. Electric bikes are a popular option to assist with the hills in this landscape.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Around Montmaurin route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views of the countryside. You'll pass by the Montmaurin Climb, providing a good workout, and cycle through the Montmaurin Centre, where you can experience the local village charm. The area is known for its green fields and small stone villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle Around Montmaurin?
faqs.answer: The region is beautiful throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be warm, but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons. Always check the local weather forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is designed to be a loop, so you can typically start and finish in Montmaurin itself. Parking is usually available within the village, though specific designated cycling parking areas may vary. Look for public parking spaces in the village centre.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small portion (approximately 10%) of this route overlaps with the official cycling path known as D'Aurignac à Gourdan.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Around Montmaurin route. It's freely accessible for public use.

faqs.question: Is the Around Montmaurin route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling with dogs can be challenging, especially on a touring bicycle route, if your dog is accustomed to running alongside your bike or can be safely carried, they are generally permitted on public roads and paths. Always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Montmaurin itself, particularly around the Montmaurin Centre, may offer small local establishments where you can find refreshments. However, it's always a good idea to carry your own water and snacks, especially on a touring route, as options can be limited in rural areas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A helmet is always advised for safety.
